seoTitle: How to Use Jira Dashboards in Confluence
metaDescription: Learn how to embed live Jira data, like issue counts and sprint status, directly into a Confluence page so your team docs stay up to date automatically.
intro: Rather than copying Jira status updates into a Confluence page by hand, you can embed live Jira data directly, so reports and project pages stay current automatically. This guide covers connecting the two tools and adding Jira content to a Confluence page.

steps.heading: Open the Confluence page you want to update
steps.body: Navigate to the specific page where you want to display Jira data.

steps.heading: Insert a Jira macro
steps.body: Use Confluence's editor to add a Jira issues or report macro to the page.

steps.heading: Connect to your Jira instance
steps.body: If prompted, authorize the connection between Confluence and Jira so the macro can pull live data.

steps.heading: Configure the Jira query
steps.body: Set up the JQL query or filter that determines which issues or data the macro displays.

steps.heading: Save and review the page
steps.body: Save the page and confirm the embedded Jira data is displaying correctly and updating as issues change.

faq.question: Does the Jira data in Confluence update automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, once embedded through a macro, the data reflects the current state of your Jira issues rather than a static snapshot.

faq.question: Do Jira and Confluence need to be connected as separate products?
faq.answer: If they're separate instances, you'll need to link them through an application link before macros can pull live data.

faq.question: Can I show a sprint's progress on a Confluence page?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure a Jira macro to display issues from a specific sprint or use a report macro for sprint-level status.

faq.question: What if the Jira macro shows no results?
faq.answer: Double-check your JQL query and confirm the connection between Confluence and Jira is properly authorized.
